markers.evidence_type: defining marker for non-inflammatory pericyte subtype

other_findings.finding: RGS5+ pericytes represent the non-inflammatory pericyte subpopulation in human skin, with lower expression of inflammatory cytokines and chemokines compared to TAGLN+/ACTA2-high pericytes
Pericytes are embedded in the basement membrane of capillaries and are identified by high expression of ACTA2, RGS5, and PDGFRB, distinguishing them from perivascular adventitial fibroblasts
Two major pericyte populations are present in human skin: RGS5+ (non-inflammatory) and TAGLN+ (inflammatory/contractile)

evidence_quotes.quote: Pericytes are a specialised mesenchymal cell population embedded in the basement membrane of capillaries... identified and distinguished from perivascular adventitial fibroblasts by high expression of ACTA2, RGS5, and PDGFRB
evidence_quotes.source_file: Ganier et al. (2024) Multiscale spatial mapping of cell populations across the skin layers

evidence_quotes.quote: Regarding perivascular cell populations, we found three populations of pericytes... there were two major pericyte populations, which we denoted RGS5+ and TAGLN+ based on high expression of those genes
evidence_quotes.source_file: Ganier et al. (2024) Multiscale spatial mapping of cell populations across the skin layers
